page_content:
CHEM460 Structure Determination Using Spectroscopic Methods (3 Credits) The use of infrared, ultraviolet-visible, proton and carbon-13 nuclear magnetic resonance and mass spectroscopy for structure determination in organic chemistry. Prerequisite: Must have completed CHEM243; or CHEM247; or (CHEM241 and CHEM242). Formerly: CHEM660.

CHEM481 Physical Chemistry I (3 Credits) Thermodynamics and kinetics of chemical and molecular systems. Topics may include internal energy, heat, work, enthalpy, entropy, free energy, and spontaneity as well as reaction order, differential rate laws, integrated rate laws, and rate laws for multi-step processes. Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in CHEM135; or minimum grade of C- in CHEM271 and CHEM272; or minimum grade of C- in CHEM276 and CHEM277. And minimum grade of C- in MATH141. And minimum grade of C- in PHYS260 and PHYS261; or minimum grade of C- in PHYS141. Credit Only Granted for: CHEM481 or CHEM480.
